series.title: [PATCH 0/7] Misc changes to allow building with Windows Clang

review.summary: The series is mostly straightforward portability cleanup, but patch 6 appears to introduce a Meson configuration regression for builds that descend into `storage-daemon/` while `have_tools` is false. The previous file handled that case by becoming a no-op; the new top-level `assert(have_tools)` would hard-fail configuration instead.

stages.A: Reviewed each patch for semantic changes versus pure cleanup and focused on build-system control-flow changes.
stages.B: Source cleanups in patches 1-5 look low risk and consistent with the stated portability goal.
stages.C: Patch 7 correctly avoids generating symbol lists when modules are disabled and looks behaviorally sound.
stages.D: Patch 6 changes `storage-daemon/meson.build` from conditionally building the executable to asserting `have_tools`, which is a configuration-surface change rather than a refactor.
stages.E: Overall risk is low except for the Meson regression; that issue is sufficient to block the series until the guard logic is corrected.

findings.title: `assert(have_tools)` can regress `--disable-tools` configurations
findings.description: Before this patch, `storage-daemon/meson.build` safely handled `have_tools == false` by skipping the `qemu-storage-daemon` target while still allowing the subdir to be parsed. The patch replaces that conditional with `assert(have_tools)`. If the top-level Meson logic still enters `storage-daemon/` when tools are disabled, configuration now fails instead of cleanly skipping the target. The patch itself does not show any corresponding change to the caller side, so this is a real regression risk for non-default build configurations.
findings.suggestion: Keep the local `if have_tools` guard around the target creation, or move the `subdir('storage-daemon')` call itself under a `have_tools` check in the parent `meson.build` in the same series. Without one of those changes, `--disable-tools` style configurations may start failing at configure time.
findings.confidence_reason: The regression follows directly from the shown diff: a conditional no-op becomes an unconditional assertion, and no compensating caller-side guard is included in the patch series.

cover_letter:
Subject: [PATCH 0/7] Misc changes to allow building with Windows Clang
From: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 18:00:26 +0200
Message-Id: <20260326160033.68318-1-kkostiuk@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

This is the first set of changes to allow building qemu-ga with Microsoft Windows Clang.
It includes some cleanups and fixes that also make the code more portable.
The main goal is to be able to build qemu-ga with the MSVC toolchain
because MinGW-w64 is not available for ARM64 Windows.

These changes were tested on CentOS Stream, Arch Linux, FreeBSD, NetBSD.
Full QEMU tree was compiled without any issues on all these platforms.

The full set of changes consists of about fifteen more patches.
Once that is complete, I will add documentation on how to build
qemu-ga with MSVC clang and a CI job that tests it.

Kostiantyn Kostiuk (6):
  meson: Use stddef.h instead of unistd.h
  qom: Use g_ascii_strcasecmp instead of strcasecmp
  osdep: Remove unused strings.h
  Remove unused dirent.h
  Remove unused sys/param.h
  meson: Build block_syms and qemu_syms only when enable_modules

Paolo Bonzini (1):
  storage-daemon: use same link arguments as other tools

 include/qemu/osdep.h       |  1 -
 meson.build                | 35 +++++++++++++++++++----------------
 qom/object.c               |  4 ++--
 storage-daemon/meson.build | 16 ++++++++--------
 util/drm.c                 |  1 -
 util/path.c                |  2 --
 6 files changed, 29 insertions(+), 30 deletions(-)

--
2.52.0
diff:
From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH 1/7] meson: Use stddef.h instead of unistd.h
From: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 18:00:27 +0200
Message-Id: <20260326160033.68318-2-kkostiuk@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

This is more cross-platform way.

Signed-off-by: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Peter Maydell <peter.maydell@linaro.org>
---
 meson.build | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/meson.build b/meson.build
index d7c4095b39..711dafa9ce 100644
--- a/meson.build
+++ b/meson.build
@@ -1060,7 +1060,7 @@ endif
 
 if not cc.compiles('''
   #include <glib.h>
-  #include <unistd.h>
+  #include <stddef.h>
 
   #define QEMU_BUILD_BUG_ON(x) \
   typedef char qemu_build_bug_on[(x)?-1:1] __attribute__((unused));
-- 
2.52.0

From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH 2/7] qom: Use g_ascii_strcasecmp instead of strcasecmp
From: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 18:00:28 +0200
Message-Id: <20260326160033.68318-3-kkostiuk@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Signed-off-by: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Peter Maydell <peter.maydell@linaro.org>
---
 qom/object.c | 4 ++--
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/qom/object.c b/qom/object.c
index ff8ede8a32..e5c0c2f53e 100644
--- a/qom/object.c
+++ b/qom/object.c
@@ -1193,8 +1193,8 @@ GSList *object_class_get_list(const char *implements_type,
 
 static gint object_class_cmp(gconstpointer a, gconstpointer b, gpointer d)
 {
-    return strcasecmp(object_class_get_name((ObjectClass *)a),
-                      object_class_get_name((ObjectClass *)b));
+    return g_ascii_strcasecmp(object_class_get_name((ObjectClass *)a),
+                              object_class_get_name((ObjectClass *)b));
 }
 
 GSList *object_class_get_list_sorted(const char *implements_type,
-- 
2.52.0

From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH 6/7] storage-daemon: use same link arguments as other tools
From: Paolo Bonzini <pbonzini@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 18:00:32 +0200
Message-Id: <20260326160033.68318-7-kkostiuk@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Signed-off-by: Paolo Bonzini <pbonzini@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Kevin Wolf <kwolf@redhat.com>
---
 meson.build                |  8 ++++----
 storage-daemon/meson.build | 16 ++++++++--------
 2 files changed, 12 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)

diff --git a/meson.build b/meson.build
index 711dafa9ce..e1baa0ea80 100644
--- a/meson.build
+++ b/meson.build
@@ -4496,15 +4496,15 @@ if xkbcommon.found()
 endif
 
 if have_tools
-  link_args = enable_modules ? ['@block.syms'] : []
+  tools_link_args = enable_modules ? ['@block.syms'] : []
   qemu_img = executable('qemu-img', [files('qemu-img.c'), hxdep],
-             link_args: link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
+             link_args: tools_link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
              dependencies: [authz, block, crypto, io, qom, qemuutil], install: true)
   qemu_io = executable('qemu-io', files('qemu-io.c'),
-             link_args: link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
+             link_args: tools_link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
              dependencies: [block, qemuutil], install: true)
   qemu_nbd = executable('qemu-nbd', files('qemu-nbd.c'),
-               link_args: link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
+               link_args: tools_link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
                dependencies: [blockdev, qemuutil, selinux],
                install: true)
 
diff --git a/storage-daemon/meson.build b/storage-daemon/meson.build
index 5e61a9d1bd..fc088cbea7 100644
--- a/storage-daemon/meson.build
+++ b/storage-daemon/meson.build
@@ -1,14 +1,14 @@
+assert(have_tools)
+
 qsd_ss = ss.source_set()
 qsd_ss.add(files('qemu-storage-daemon.c'))
 qsd_ss.add(blockdev, chardev, qmp, qom, qemuutil)
 
 subdir('qapi')
 
-if have_tools
-  qsd_ss = qsd_ss.apply({})
-  qsd = executable('qemu-storage-daemon',
-                   qsd_ss.sources(),
-                   link_args: '@block.syms', link_depends: block_syms,
-                   dependencies: qsd_ss.dependencies(),
-                   install: true)
-endif
+qsd_ss = qsd_ss.apply({})
+qsd = executable('qemu-storage-daemon',
+                 qsd_ss.sources(),
+                 link_args: tools_link_args, link_depends: block_syms,
+                 dependencies: qsd_ss.dependencies(),
+                 install: true)
-- 
2.52.0

From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH 7/7] meson: Build block_syms and qemu_syms only when
 enable_modules
From: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 18:00:33 +0200
Message-Id: <20260326160033.68318-8-kkostiuk@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Signed-off-by: Kostiantyn Kostiuk <kkostiuk@redhat.com>
---
 meson.build | 25 ++++++++++++++-----------
 1 file changed, 14 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

diff --git a/meson.build b/meson.build
index e1baa0ea80..c7ab9c47c4 100644
--- a/meson.build
+++ b/meson.build
@@ -3897,6 +3897,9 @@ modinfo_generate = find_program('scripts/modinfo-generate.py')
 modinfo_files = []
 audio_modinfo_files = []
 
+block_syms = []
+qemu_syms = []
+
 block_mods = []
 system_mods = []
 emulator_modules = []
@@ -4012,18 +4015,18 @@ if enable_modules
   if emulator_modules.length() > 0
     alias_target('modules', emulator_modules)
   endif
-endif
 
-nm = find_program('nm')
-undefsym = find_program('scripts/undefsym.py')
-block_syms = custom_target('block.syms', output: 'block.syms',
-                             input: [libqemuutil, block_mods],
-                             capture: true,
-                             command: [undefsym, nm, '@INPUT@'])
-qemu_syms = custom_target('qemu.syms', output: 'qemu.syms',
-                             input: [libqemuutil, system_mods],
-                             capture: true,
-                             command: [undefsym, nm, '@INPUT@'])
+  nm = find_program('nm')
+  undefsym = find_program('scripts/undefsym.py')
+  block_syms = custom_target('block.syms', output: 'block.syms',
+                              input: [libqemuutil, block_mods],
+                              capture: true,
+                              command: [undefsym, nm, '@INPUT@'])
+  qemu_syms = custom_target('qemu.syms', output: 'qemu.syms',
+                              input: [libqemuutil, system_mods],
+                              capture: true,
+                              command: [undefsym, nm, '@INPUT@'])
+endif
 
 authz_ss = authz_ss.apply({})
 libauthz = static_library('authz', authz_ss.sources() + genh,
-- 
2.52.0
disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
